Class JsonRpcFaultException

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Throwable
java.lang.Exception
org.apache.axis2.json.rpc.JsonRpcFaultException
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class JsonRpcFaultException extends Exception
Exception thrown by JSON-RPC service methods to signal a structured error with a specific HTTP status code.

How it flows through the stack

  1. Service method (e.g. FinancialBenchmarkService.portfolioVariance()) throws this on input validation failure
  2. Method.invoke() wraps it in InvocationTargetException
  3. invalid reference 
    JsonRpcMessageReceiver#invokeService
    unwraps the ITE, detects this type, and sets:
    • Constants.HTTP_RESPONSE_STATE → the HTTP status (e.g. 422)
    • JsonConstant.RETURN_OBJECT → the Axis2JsonErrorResponse
    The response then flows through the normal JSON formatter path (not the SOAP fault path), producing a clean JSON error envelope.

Why a checked exception instead of return-value error patterns

Returning XxxResponse.failed(msg) with HTTP 200 made it impossible for intermediaries (proxies, MCP clients) to distinguish errors from success without parsing the body. Throwing produces proper HTTP semantics: 422 for validation, 429 for rate-limit, 503 for unavailability.

Usage in a service method: 

if (request.getWeights() == null) {
    throw JsonRpcFaultException.validationError(
        "Missing required field: \"weights\" array");
}
See Also:
  • Axis2JsonErrorResponse
  • invalid reference 
    JsonRpcMessageReceiver#invokeService
